title: Add Settings Page with Language, Version, Admin, About App, and Download sections

requirements.text: Create a dedicated Settings page (frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx) accessible from the app navigation. The page must contain clearly separated sections: Language, Version, Admin, About App, and Download App.

quotes: Language ko settings me rakha hai and version and admin and about app download

acceptanceCriteria: A Settings page exists and is reachable via a navigation link (sidebar and/or bottom nav).
The page renders five distinct sections: Language, Version, Admin, About App, and Download App.
The page follows the existing dark-mode-first design system (CSS custom properties, Tailwind tokens).

requirements.text: In the Settings page Language section, embed the existing LanguageSelector component (frontend/src/components/LanguageSelector.tsx) so users can switch the app language from within Settings. Remove or hide the LanguageSelector from any other location where it currently appears (e.g., top navigation bar) to avoid duplication.

quotes: Language ko settings me rakha hai

acceptanceCriteria: The Language section in Settings shows the LanguageSelector dropdown.
Selecting a language updates the app's LanguageContext and persists to localStorage.
The LanguageSelector is no longer duplicated in the top navigation bar or other areas.

requirements.text: In the Settings page Version section, display the current app version string (e.g., 'v1.0.0' or a hardcoded build version) as a read-only informational row with a label 'App Version' and the version value.
acceptanceCriteria: The Version section displays a label 'App Version' and a version string.
The version info is read-only (no editable fields).

requirements.text: In the Settings page Admin section, show an 'Admin Dashboard' navigation link that is only visible to users for whom useIsCallerAdmin returns true. Unauthenticated users or non-admin users must not see this row. Clicking the link navigates to the existing Admin Dashboard page.
acceptanceCriteria: The Admin section row is hidden for non-admin and unauthenticated users.
For admin users, the row is visible and clicking it navigates to the Admin Dashboard.
The admin check uses the existing useIsCallerAdmin hook.

requirements.text: In the Settings page About App section, display static informational content about the Mediatube app: app name, a short description, links to the Privacy Policy and Copyright Policy pages, and contact/support info. This section must be visible to all users.
acceptanceCriteria: The About App section displays the app name 'Mediatube' and a short description.
Links to Copyright Policy (existing CopyrightPolicyPage) are present and functional.
The section is visible to all users regardless of authentication state.

requirements.text: In the Settings page Download App section, display a button or link that navigates to the existing Download App page (frontend/src/pages/DownloadAppPage.tsx). The row should show platform icons (Android/iOS) and a label 'Download Mediatube App'.
acceptanceCriteria: The Download section row shows a 'Download Mediatube App' label with Android and iOS indicators.
Clicking the row navigates to the existing DownloadAppPage.
The section is visible to all users.

requirements.text: Register the Settings page as a new route (e.g., /settings) in the TanStack Router route tree in frontend/src/App.tsx, and add a 'Settings' entry to the sidebar navigation in frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx and to the bottom navigation bar in frontend/src/components/BottomNav.tsx using an appropriate settings icon.

quotes: Language ko settings me rakha hai and version and admin and about app download

acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /settings renders the Settings page.
A Settings link with an icon appears in the sidebar.
The active route is correctly highlighted when on the Settings page.

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
The LanguageSelector component must not be rewritten — compose it as-is within the Settings page.
Admin section visibility must be gated strictly by useIsCallerAdmin — no client-side workarounds.
Do not add any new backend endpoints; all Settings page content is frontend-only.
nonGoals: Adding new backend logic or data models for settings.
Persisting any settings other than language (already handled by LanguageContext).
Building a notification settings or account security section.
Modifying the existing DownloadAppPage or AdminDashboardPage content.
